fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 38-year-old White male with schizophrenia for routine visit.
fields.Subjective: Patient arrived appearing casual and cooperative. Reports taking quetiapine 400 mg at bedtime consistently using evening alarm. States doing well. Experiences very occasional whispers maybe once monthly which he dismisses. No other hallucinations or paranoid thoughts. Sleep good at 7-8 hours nightly. Appetite healthy, weight stable at 179 lbs. Working part-time at library shelving books, enjoys quiet environment. Mood 'pretty good' rated 7/10. Lives with mother which provides stability. Enjoys reading history books and documentaries. Attends support group monthly. One year sober from alcohol. Good relationship with mother. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Rachel Peters, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, good grooming, calm demeanor

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and CMP within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with mother; working part-time; one year sober; engaged in hobbies and support group.
Assessment_and_Plan: Good medication adherence and symptom control.
fields.Author: Note written by Rachel Peters, PMHNP-BC, Psychiatric Nurse Practitioner — Quiet Minds Wellness, 4521 Library Lane, Portland, OR 97214, Phone: (555) 667-3329, Fax: (555) 667-3330
